commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=524fd92ad3ff1d5e4b25e0418bde5331e85ffea2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `emitThinkingField` option to the shared DeepSeek V4 OpenAI-compatible stream wrapper, opts the DeepSeek plugin out of sending `payload.thinking`, and updates DeepSeek/shared payload-shaping tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defect: source inspection shows the new opt-out is wired to `provider === "deepseek"`, while the claimed Foundry/OpenAI-compatible route is handled by the post-plugin fallback. The original live 400 is plausible but not independently reproducible from the attached PR evidence because no runtime artifact is inspectable.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof and a maintainer-visible provider routing revision; ClawSweeper should not take over while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(foundry-deepseek): stop sending unsupported thinking payload
target.body:
## Summary
- stop sending `payload.thinking` for the Foundry-hosted DeepSeek V4 route we reproduced locally
- keep Xiaomi/MiMo on the existing native `thinking` field behavior
- keep `reasoning_effort` mapping when thinking is enabled
- preserve the helper default behavior unless a caller opts out explicitly

## Why
This PR is now intentionally scoped to the **Foundry-hosted DeepSeek V4 OpenAI-compatible route** because that is the only route I reproduced locally.

Local repro before fix on `microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro` failed with:
- `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking`

My earlier wording overstated the proof surface by implying native DeepSeek API behavior was verified. It was not.

## What this patch actually changes
- the shared helper now supports an explicit opt-out of `payload.thinking`
- the current opt-out is wired at the DeepSeek plugin call site
- Xiaomi/MiMo keep their prior `payload.thinking` behavior

## Verification
### Real runtime proof
After the local runtime patch, these succeeded and returned `OK`:
- `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking off --message 'Reply with exactly OK' --json`
- `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high --message 'Reply with exactly OK' --json`

### Scope caveat
I do **not** have direct live proof for the native `deepseek/...` provider from this Telegram-bound main session because direct DeepSeek model override was rejected by local agent policy. So this PR should be reviewed as a fix for the **Foundry-proven route**, not as verified native DeepSeek API behavior.

## Follow-up to prior PR
This supersedes the earlier narrower framing in #85683. That PR is closed in favor of this one.
target.author: silvesterxm
